title: carthamus dentatus subsp. ruber
intro: Carthamus dentatus subsp. ruber, also known as the red safflower, is a plant species within the daisy family, characterized by its vibrant red flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains a high concentration of carotenoids and flavonoids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving heart health. The herbal preparations of Carthamus dentatus subsp. ruber include infusions, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to treat conditions like arthritis, high cholesterol, and certain types of cancer. Additionally, the plant's oil has been traditionally used in skincare products due to its moisturizing and anti-aging properties.
